behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality (explicit-image detection), so a stub would hide real misses (LLM-DEP yes). Real OS/network behavior is not the core risk for this behavior. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes a single moderation decision path, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run submissions with a labeled explicit-image eval set against the real vision model and assert each sample is held for review and never reaches publish state. Include assertion on downstream hold/publish state transition after the model result.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model robustness to obfuscation variants; deterministic fakes always pass and miss true failures (LLM-DEP yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow: this is one model-classification decision per post, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a corpus of hate-speech samples with obfuscation variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, mixed unicode normalization forms) and call the real text model. Assert posts are flagged and routed to review for each variant.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (image result + text result -> publish path). The phrase "passes both content checks" disqualifies model-quality testing; we can stub pass outcomes and still catch the real failure (drop/hold bug). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, persistence/queue layer, and publish path in-process. Stub both classifiers to return pass and assert exactly one published record/event is emitted, with no hold record and no dropped state.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is end-to-end latency under real infra (real HTTP/model endpoints, serialization, queueing, process scheduling). In-process fakes can hide timeout/latency regressions (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Agentic/Workflow: assertion is timing SLO, not model decision quality; behavior does not require validating semantic correctness of model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end performance test with real service deployment and real model endpoints (or production-equivalent networked services). Submit representative mixed posts at peak-like load and assert p95/p99 completion for the full pipeline remains <=5s from submission to final decision.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real unavailability modes at an external boundary (timeouts, connection failures, 5xx), which are often misrepresented by simple mocks; real HTTP/subprocess/network behavior matters (REAL-INFRA yes). Rejected Agentic: this tests outage handling, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with the vision dependency made unavailable via real failure injection (endpoint down, network block, or real 503/timeout responses). Assert image posts enter retry queue with correct retry metadata and are never auto-approved during outage window.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic decisions in sequence across repeated submissions; consistency can fail due stochastic model outputs and cross-request handoff/caching policy (LLM-DEP yes, MULTI-STEP yes). Single-call Agentic is insufficient because requirement is temporal consistency across repeated runs.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target short window through the full pipeline using the real vision model. Assert final moderation decision remains consistent across attempts and matches the system’s consistency policy (e.g., cache/sticky decision behavior) for that window.
